title: The Big Picture: Domain Coloring & Conformal Maps
description: A complex function maps a plane to a plane — a literal graph would need 4 dimensions. This opener introduces the two substitute pictures used all through the subject: domain coloring (color each input by the angle of its output) and side-by-side input/output planes.

title: Grid Deformation Under f(z) = z²
description: Watch a square grid deform under z². Vertical and horizontal lines map to two families of parabolas that stay perpendicular everywhere — except at the origin, where f′(0) = 0 and conformality visibly breaks down.

title: Roots of Unity
description: The n solutions of zⁿ = 1 sit at the vertices of a regular n-gon on the unit circle. Explore how polar form turns multiplication into rotate-and-scale, and why raising each root to the nth power walks it around the circle back to 1.

title: Open Sets Explorer
description: The topology vocabulary of ℂ, made draggable: test points with ε-disks to see what interior, boundary, open, and closed actually mean — the definitions every later theorem quietly relies on.

title: Conformality & the Jacobian
description: Why do holomorphic maps preserve angles? Zoom in at a point and see the Jacobian act as a pure rotation-plus-scaling when the Cauchy-Riemann equations hold — the geometric content of complex differentiability.

title: The Complex Logarithm & Its Branch Cut
description: log z is multivalued: every loop around the origin adds 2πi. Explore the spiral of values, choose a branch, and see exactly what a branch cut removes to force a single-valued function.

title: Geometric Series: Partial Sums in the Plane
description: Drag z and watch the partial sums of Σzⁿ trace a path: a spiral forced inward to 1/(1−z) when |z| < 1, an unbounded outward spiral when |z| > 1, and endless circling right on the boundary. An ε-disk around the limit makes the ε–N definition of convergence literal — find the N past which the path never leaves.

title: Contour Deformation & the Cauchy Integral Formula
description: Two panels: deform integration contours freely without changing the integral (as long as no singularity is crossed), then step through the actual derivation of the Cauchy Integral Formula, including the shrinking-circle limit that makes it work.

title: From CIF to the Fundamental Theorem of Algebra
description: A single proof chain, animated end to end: the Cauchy Integral Formula gives derivative formulas, which give Cauchy's estimates, which give Liouville's theorem, which proves that every nonconstant polynomial has a root. Each link pairs a visualization with a step-through proof. Lesson 5 embeds a shorter scrolly recap of this same chain (9 steps) — this standalone page is the long-form version.

title: Isolated Zeros — the Floor Argument
description: Why continuity forces g(z) away from 0 near z₀, so a zero of a holomorphic function can never have another zero arbitrarily close by. Drag z and watch the reverse-triangle-inequality floor hold live, synced to the general proof it's demonstrating.

title: Vary the Circle — Why R = 3, Exactly
description: Deriving, not asserting, that a power series' radius of convergence equals the distance to the nearest singularity. Grow a certifying circle toward the singularity for the R≥3 half; watch |f(z)| blow up approaching it for the R≤3 half.

title: Singularity Zoo — Domain Coloring
description: Three isolated singularities, same domain-coloring convention, one shared zoom slider: removable stays smooth, a pole brightens to a clean double-wound white center, and an essential singularity cycles through every color at every zoom level — Casorati-Weierstrass made visible.

title: The Crosscut Construction
description: Turning a region with holes into one connected boundary, so Cauchy's theorem applies. Shrink the crosscut's width to zero and watch the two opposite-direction traversals cancel exactly — an orientation-reversal identity, not a limiting argument.
